title: PM Workfront Snapshot-Review Drillthrough
objective: Add a focused read-only PM workfront path from PM-review rows with a submitted progress snapshot for the same workpackage into the existing PM approval snapshot-review screen, preserving return context back to PM workfront and zero mutation behavior.
orchestration_inputs: PM Lane 012 remains the deployment gate for hosted mutation-seam parity and live PM proof.
The coordinator selected workfront-to-approval snapshot-review drillthrough as the next local PM ergonomics slice after PM Lane 024.
Read-only product scout 019e2cd2-e992-7a01-b17e-e57c381159e9 confirmed snapshot-review drillthrough is the natural bounded Lane 025 candidate and requires no approval route or backend change.

Read-only governance scout 019e2cd2-e9cf-7e53-967c-0006a731a9ed supplied the packet, handoff, validation, and host parity closeout scaffold; the coordinator narrowed its suggested grouping lane to the concrete snapshot-review drillthrough scope.
Coordinator execution kept write ownership local to operations-web frontend, focused browser proof, and governance surfaces while scouts ran as parallel read-only lanes.
constrained_outputs: PM workfront now reads existing submitted progress snapshots through /api/v1/reads/snapshots without creating a new endpoint.
PM-review rows with a submitted snapshot for the same workpackage now expose a focused Review snapshot link.
The link routes to /pm-review/approval with screen=snapshot-review, detailId=<snapshot id>, returnTo=/pm-review/workfront, and returnLabel=PM workfront.

The focused PM workfront smoke asserts the Review snapshot href, clicks into approval snapshot-review, verifies Progress Snapshot rendering, verifies scoped decision-history?entity_id=<snapshot id>&limit=25, verifies Return to PM workfront, and preserves zero-mutation posture.

Existing read-only advisory, schedule drillthrough, escalation approval drillthrough, task approval drillthrough, workpackage approval drillthrough, decision-history drillthrough, lens filters, and PM-clicked return-to-lead proof remain intact.
